mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) during periods of rising inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4% signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor markets are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 110 signals a strengthening supply chain, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) during periods of strengthening supply chains.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1200 signals a weakening demand, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in weakening demand periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the past 3 months, combined with a decline in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) below 2% over the past 6 months, signals a tightening monetary cycle and lower inflation expectations, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1% over 3 months and CPIAUCSL < 2% over 6 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) during periods of tightening monetary cycles and lower inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 150,000 signals a weakening labor market, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in weakening labor markets are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in real disposable personal income (RSAFS) over the past 6 months, combined with a decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4%, signals a strengthening demand and labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) during periods of strengthening demand and labor markets.
